name: Laz

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Laz (lazz1240).

notes: Skeleton. Laz is a Kartvelian (South Caucasian) language written here in the Georgian Mkhedruli script, a cleanly phonemic alphabet. All 33 standard Georgian letters are mapped to their phonemic values, including the three-way stop/affricate contrasts of voiced, aspirated and ejective (e.g. კ kʼ, ქ kʰ, გ ɡ; წ tsʼ, ც tsʰ, ძ dz) and the uvular ejective ყ. The extra Laz letter ჷ writes the schwa vowel ə. Word stress is not marked in the script and is left unencoded; otherwise grapheme-to-phoneme mapping is close to complete for the alphabet.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Laz (lazz1240)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
